career_angle: Modal is genuinely useful for ML engineers who need serverless GPU compute without Kubernetes headaches. If you're building AI products, learn their SDK -- but wait a week post-1.0 for the inevitable patch releases before migrating production workloads.

dimensions.name: Unknown
dimensions.rationale: Company blog announcing own product -- inherently self-serving source, though Modal is a real VC-backed infra company with paying customers

dimensions.name: Unknown
dimensions.rationale: Zero benchmarks, no migration stats, no customer testimonials cited in the summary. 'Maturity and stability' with no metrics is vibes-based versioning

dimensions.name: Unknown
dimensions.rationale: It's an open client library -- anyone can pip install and verify the 1.0 claim. The software exists and works

dimensions.name: Unknown
dimensions.rationale: Maximum conflict -- company announcing its own product milestone to drive adoption. This is marketing, full stop

dimensions.name: Unknown
dimensions.rationale: Modal has genuine developer mindshare in the ML infra space, but 1.0 version bumps are often arbitrary marketing decisions rather than technical milestones

dimensions.name: Unknown
dimensions.rationale: Extremely vague -- 'maturity and stability' without defining what changed, what breaking changes were resolved, or what stability guarantees 1.0 actually provides

dimensions.name: Unknown
dimensions.rationale: Modal has shipped consistently and has real enterprise customers. Their pre-1.0 product was already production-grade for many users
